---
title: "The Autoloan Lake Is Full (And the Site Is Live)"
date: 2026-06-04
description: "Finished the ABS-EE autoloan backfill on a Hetzner VM, launched Ben's Field Notes, and started the other asset classes in the background."
categories: [structured-finance, consumer-credit, methodology]
type: notebook
---

After a few weeks of downloading XML from EDGAR on a Hetzner box that cost about as much
as a burrito bowl per day, the autoloan panel is complete: every filing month from
**201611** through the current month, loan-level Parquet on R2, no gaps.

This post is the public version of that milestone — and the announcement that
**[Ben's Field Notes](https://github.com/benuehlinger/bens-field-notes)** is live.

## What shipped

**Data lake (private):**

- Full ABS-EE **autoloan** history — every filing month from 2016 through present
- Monthly ingest catalogs and a VM runbook so backfills don't depend on my laptop staying awake

**This site:**

- [Auto Loan Credit — ABS-EE Panel](../../dashboards/abs-ee.qmd) — delinquency and pool stats from the lake
- [Bank Lending Standards — SLOOS](../../dashboards/sloos.qmd) — Fed senior loan officer survey
- Background on [what ABS-EE is](../../data-sources.qmd) if you're new here

Charts are pre-aggregated at deploy time and rendered as interactive Observable Plot
visualizations. The R2 bucket stays private; data is embedded into the page at build time.

## What's running next

Autoloan was the first Schedule AL class because the schema is well understood and the
filings are manageable on a 2 GB VM with swap.

The same pipeline now supports the other Reg AB II asset classes: auto leases, commercial
mortgages, residential mortgages, and debt securities. Autolease is running next —
watching storage growth before committing to RMBS, where individual filings can be gigabytes.

## How to read the autoloan dashboard

The live dashboard aggregates servicer-reported loan status across every trust that filed
in a given month. It's a **sample of securitized auto credit**, not the whole US auto market.

Useful for:

- Cross-issuer delinquency trends in filed pools
- Comparing filing volume and coverage month to month

Not useful for:

- Bank-held loans that never hit a securitization trust
- Real-time credit decisions (filings lag the reporting period by weeks)

More notes when there's something worth writing — or when RMBS finishes and I need
a place to complain about XML file sizes.
